signals.title: NZD/USD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Geopolitical Stress
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5867 — USD outperformance accelerates as US-Iran Gulf escalation triggers safe-haven funding stress (DXY vs SPX corr z=+1.72σ). NZD remains the G8 laggard with a -0.842% weekly average, while the Fed 3.75% vs RBNZ 2.25% (+150bp) carry advantage favors USD. Real rate differential (+1.23% USD vs -0.83% NZD) further penalizes NZD longs. Sustained break below 0.5850 targets 2024 lows.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Intervention Risk + Options Skew
signals.text: USD/JPY at 160.04 — Price is testing the 160.00 psychological barrier previously associated with BoJ verbal warnings and intervention. Despite a +292bp OIS carry advantage for USD, the options market is pricing downside protection with a -1.18 25d RR (moderate put skew). JPY COT LF net short -86,249 remains crowded; a break above 160.50 increases the risk of immediate MoF action.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Volatility + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7131 — COT LF net long +58,041 (76.1%) indicates crowded institutional positioning while retail is 72% short, creating a sharp divergence. AUD IV at 16.1% vs HV30 at 8.1% (VRP +8.0%) signals extreme hedging demand ahead of RBA Governor Bullock's appearance. Negative CESI (-7) and geopolitical risk-off flows threaten the +70bp AUD carry advantage. Watch for retail capitulation if 0.7100 breaks.

signals.title: USD/CHF — Volatility Premium + CB Divergence
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.7920 — IV at 31.3% vs HV30 at 6.5% creates a massive +24.8% VRP, indicating the options market is pricing a significant tail event. Fed 3.75% vs SNB 0% (+375bp) carry sustains the USD bid, while the SNB market bias remains for a cut. Real rate differential (+1.23% USD vs -0.31% CHF) supports structural upside. Watch for SNB meeting on 18 Jun to confirm the dovish path.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Data Outperformance vs Carry Headwinds
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1600 — Positive CESI (+7) and resilient growth in the Eurozone provide a marginal floor, but the +172bp OIS carry advantage for USD remains the dominant driver. Options market shows mild caution with a -0.34 25d RR (put skew) and elevated VRP (+3.1%). Fed's Logan signaling a slower return to 2% inflation reinforces the 'higher for longer' USD regime. Watch 1.1580 support level.
